author: chatgpt-codex-connector
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Add printer power-cut tooling to failure detection**

Obico/OctoEverywhere can detect visual print failures, but for multi-hour prints they do not by themselves define how the printer is de-energized when a heater, MOSFET, or job goes bad. Add the missing power-control layer next to the camera stack: OctoPrint PSU Control / Octo Fire Guard for OctoPrint, or Moonraker `[power]` devices for Klipper, so alerts can actually cut power rather than only notify ([OctoPrint power plugins](https://plugins.octoprint.org/topics/power_control/), [Moonraker power docs](https://moonraker.readthedocs.io/en/latest/configuration/), [Octo Fire Guard](https://plugins.octoprint.org/plugins/octo_fire_guard/)).

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
